disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Acute solid food bolus (chicken) lodged in the mid-chest after eating, will not pass up or down for 2 hours
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I was eating chicken at dinner and a piece stopped dead in the middle of my chest about two hours ago. It won't go up and it won't go down.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Complete obstruction — unable to swallow saliva/liquids, actively drooling and spitting secretions (aspiration risk)
trigger_question_examples: Can you swallow your own saliva or sips of water?
Are you able to keep any fluid down?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No — I can't swallow anything now, not even my own spit. I have to dribble the saliva into a cup, and a sip of water just came straight back up.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Severe constant retrosternal chest pain accompanying the impaction
trigger_question_examples: Is there any pain with it?
Where is the pain and how bad is it?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes, it's a bad gripping pain right behind my breastbone and it hasn't eased at all.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Breathing currently unaffected — no noisy breathing, no voice change, speaking in full sentences (airway patent at present)
trigger_question_examples: Is your breathing okay? Any noisy breathing or change in your voice?
Are you struggling to breathe at all?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: My breathing feels alright at the moment and my voice sounds normal — it's the swallowing and the pain that are the problem.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Chronic history of intermittent solid-food dysphagia over the past year (food traversing slowly / briefly catching, self-resolving until now)
trigger_question_examples: Has food ever felt like it sticks or goes down slowly before this?
Have you had trouble swallowing solids in the past?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Now that you ask, over the past year solid food has sometimes felt like it goes down slowly or catches for a moment, but it's always cleared by itself before tonight.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Heartburn/reflux symptoms refractory to daily high-dose proton pump inhibitor therapy
trigger_question_examples: Do you get heartburn or reflux, and do your tablets help?
Are you on anything for your stomach, and is it working?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I take a strong stomach-acid tablet every day for heartburn, but it hasn't made any difference to the swallowing trouble.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Strong atopic background — allergic rhinitis and childhood eczema
trigger_question_examples: Do you have any allergies, hay fever, asthma or eczema?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes, I get bad hay fever and I had eczema as a kid.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No unintentional weight loss; prior episodes intermittent and self-limiting (pertinent negative against malignant stricture)
trigger_question_examples: Have you lost any weight without trying?
Has the swallowing been getting steadily and relentlessly worse?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, I haven't lost weight, and it's only ever been occasional and brief before — nothing steadily getting worse.

patient_initiated_exchanges.trigger_condition: AI has not yet given clear advice, or suggests waiting at home
patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: Should I just drink lots of water to try and wash it down, or will it clear on its own if I wait?
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Must advise strongly AGAINST drinking to force it down (regurgitation and aspiration risk in a complete blockage) and against waiting at home; must direct urgent Emergency Department attendance for endoscopic removal.
